covered_earnings_window: Monday, June 15, 2026 after U.S. market close and Tuesday, June 16, 2026 before U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: Risk appetite was positive during the June 15 session: reports cited rising stock futures, lower oil on U.S.-Iran peace-deal hopes, chip/AI strength, bitcoin rebound, and the upcoming Wednesday Fed meeting. The window is small-cap and mid-cap heavy, so liquidity, positioning, pre-announcements, filing quality, and management tone are unusually important.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 16, 2026 before market open; call 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.key_risk_to_call: AI licensing lumpiness or no FY lift
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Wiley has the cleanest positive setup because Research/AI momentum, margin expansion, free cash flow, and buybacks line up against a modest-growth revenue bar.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Consensus shown by MarketBeat is EPS $1.65 and revenue $450.0 million for Q4 FY2026.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Research Publishing growth, AI/data licensing traction, high-end EPS/margin delivery, and buyback support.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: AI revenue proves lumpy, Learning softness offsets Research, or FY2027 commentary is not strong enough.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: No robust public implied-move read found; event risk appears more fundamental than options-driven.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Evidence points to high-end FY2026 adjusted EBITDA margin and EPS guidance after Q3, with limited public analyst count.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; webcast June 16 at 11:30 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Revenue growth and free cash flow
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: High Tide leans up because Q1 showed record revenue, positive free cash flow, and adjusted EBITDA growth, while Q2 expectations remain cautious.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Public estimates show roughly a small loss expectation; Benzinga cited a $0.02 loss on $124.36 million revenue, while other data sources differ by currency and reporting convention.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Cabana Club growth, same-store momentum, positive free cash flow, and EBITDA margin stability.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Canadian cannabis pricing, margin compression, FX/reporting confusion, or risk-off small-cap tape.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Limited reliable options data found; cannabis retail flows can reverse quickly.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Analyst previews remain cautious despite Q1 profitability and FCF improvement.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Dave & Buster's has downside risk because the market needs proof that comp-store sales and EBITDA have turned after weak FY2025 results and downward estimate revisions.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: MarketBeat shows EPS $0.60 and revenue $580.6 million; another preview cited EPS $0.66 and revenue $578.38 million with negative EPS/revenue revisions.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Same-store sales improve, remodels and new games work, and management validates FY2026 FCF/EBITDA recovery.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Comps remain soft, EBITDA quality disappoints, leverage/consumer weakness dominates, or Q1 enthusiasm was priced into the recent bounce.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Shares had recently bounced from depressed levels, increasing two-sided reaction risk.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Recent preview data cited no upward EPS revisions and multiple downward EPS/revenue revisions over three months.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 4:30 p.m. ET
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: RFIL has a constructive operating setup because Q1 showed margin improvement, EBITDA growth, and a larger backlog, but the stock has already rerated sharply.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: MarketBeat shows EPS $0.09 and revenue $19.67 million; Q1 sales were about $19 million with gross-margin and backlog improvement.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Backlog converts, gross margin holds above historical levels, and telecom/data-center demand commentary stays firm.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Sequential revenue softness, customer timing, or valuation compression after a large move.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Liquidity and microcap volatility matter more than clean options signals.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Sparse analyst coverage; public estimates imply modest revenue growth and EPS improvement.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_table.pass_agreement: A/B/C Lean Up, wide risk spread
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Quantum leans up on a positive revenue preannouncement above prior guidance, but the event is partly de-risked and the balance sheet/loss profile keeps reversal risk high.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Quantum preannounced about $77.5 million of Q4 revenue plus or minus $2 million, above prior $68 million plus or minus $2 million guidance; MarketBeat shows expected revenue $77.5 million and EPS -$0.33.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Preannounced revenue flows through to EBITDA, cash burn is contained, and AI-era data-management commentary improves backlog confidence.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Preannouncement already captured the upside, cash remains thin, GAAP losses/expenses disappoint, or guidance lacks follow-through.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Stock had already moved sharply intraday, raising event-volatility unwind risk.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus likely reset toward the $77.5 million preannouncement, limiting surprise power.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_table.evidence_quality: High for timing, mixed for setup
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Domo leans down because the rescheduled release and NT 10-Q extension create a quality-risk overhang, even though prior billings and margin trends improved.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Public estimates show roughly EPS -$0.12 to -$0.14 and about $79.75 million revenue; prior Q4 revenue was $79.6 million and billings grew 8%.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Delay proves administrative only, billings/RPO keep improving, retention rises, and guidance supports a profitability inflection.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Financial-statement delay raises confidence concerns, growth remains near flat, or FY2027 guide fails to show acceleration.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Filing-delay news can dominate normal earnings positioning.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Domo has a history of EPS beats, but the market may care more about clean filing, billings, and FY2027 growth.

ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Vince has some recovery evidence from FY2025 sales and EBITDA improvement, but Q1 seasonality, small size, and thin liquidity make the edge too fragile for a stronger call.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: TradingView cited Q1 revenue estimate $63.05 million and EPS -$0.19; MarketBeat shows the prior quarter beat EPS and revenue.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Brand demand holds, gross margin stays firm, and FY2026 sales/EBITDA commentary validates the turnaround.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Q1 loss is worse than feared, retail demand softens, or Saks-related/working-capital issues linger.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: No reliable implied-move read found; liquidity and spread risk are material.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Sparse estimate coverage; sources differ on EPS expectations.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: June 16, 2026 before U.S. market open; call 8:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Uxin's used-car unit and revenue growth are real, but thin ADS trading, losses, low gross margin, and China ADR risk leave no clean directional edge.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Uxin scheduled June 16 before-open results; FY2025 retail transaction volume rose 134.7% and revenue rose 78.6%, but gross margin was only 6.7% and adjusted EBITDA remained negative.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Retail volume and revenue beat guidance, superstore ramp improves, and losses narrow.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Gross margin compression, higher sales expense, cash needs, or China ADR sentiment overwhelms growth.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: No reliable implied-move data found; liquidity and ADR risk are primary positioning concerns.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Traditional U.S. consensus coverage is limited; company KPIs matter more than EPS consensus.

qualified_names.why: Score above 30, reversal risk below 40, low disparity, high evidence quality.
Score above 30, reversal risk just below 40, good operating momentum, moderate evidence quality.
Remaining names failed reversal-risk, confidence, or evidence-quality filters.

excluded_names.reason: Score above 30 but reversal risk above 40 after a sharp rerating and thin liquidity.
Score above 30 but reversal risk high because the revenue beat was preannounced and the stock already reacted.
Bearish score strong but reversal risk above 40 due low-bar turnaround relief risk.
Bearish score strong but reversal risk high because billings/RPO could offset filing-delay fears.
Absolute score below allocation threshold and reversal risk above 40.
Neutral score, low confidence, and reversal risk above 40.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

consensus_method_note: Disparity is a 0-100 measure of disagreement across three independent passes. WLY and HITI had low disparity and qualified for allocation. RFIL, QMCO, PLAY, and DOMO had directionally useful consensus but reversal risk was too high for allocation. VNCE and UXIN stayed near Neutral because the passes did not produce enough conviction.
disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
